title: Govt flags AI-driven identity fraud risks in banking, fintech sector: What you can do to protect your money
summary: The central government has warned that deepfakes and synthetic identities are increasingly being used to target digital verification systems. Banks and fintech firms have been asked to strengthen onboarding and fraud-detection frameworks.

title: Are gifts received at wedding exempt from taxation? Here's what newlyweds should know
summary: Indian weddings involve cash, gold, and gifts, which may raise tax concerns for newlyweds. The income tax law allows exemptions for gifts received during marriage, but they must still be disclosed as income. Details here.

title: Does your health insurance cover oxygen therapy, nursing and home care? Check your policy for hidden gaps
summary: Whether it is an ageing parent requiring months of specialised nursing post-surgery, a stroke survivor needing extensive physical rehabilitation, or a patient recovering from severe respiratory illness relying on supplemental oxygen, long-term care takes a toll.

title: Can you use stock market losses to reduce tax on other capital gains? Income tax rules explained
summary: Investors can offset equity-related losses against other capital gains to lower tax liabilities. The adjustment depends on the nature of the loss and the type of capital gain. Details here.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Minimum pay, allowances, salary hike, increment — Here are top demands from Railway employee groups
summary: Two representative groups — Indian Railways Technical Supervisors Association and Railways Senior Citizens Welfare Society — are seeking better pay levels, salary structure, annual increments, DA hike and allowances for employees and pensioners.

title: Think a 20% market correction is rare? 45 years of Sensex data says otherwise
summary: The Sensex has witnessed an average intra-year drawdown of 20% since 1980, yet nearly 80% of calendar years ended with positive returns.

title: Missed your credit card payment by 1 day? Here’s what happens to your credit score

The rocket company's IPO positions it among the largest US-listed firms, with Musk reiterating his ambition to take humanity to Mars and beyond.

title: Global stocks: Shell pauses $3 billion share buyback
summary: Shell has decided to put its $3 billion share repurchase program on hold from June 12 to July 14, as it navigates the implications of its acquisition of ARC Resources. During this period, shares that are not bought back will be earmarked for upcoming repurchase efforts.

title: Sebi weighs introducing long-term futures and options contracts: Tuhin Kanta Pandey
summary: Sebi is evaluating longer-term futures and options contracts, broader commodity derivatives and bond index derivatives to deepen markets. The regulator highlighted resilient capital markets, strong domestic investor participation and a robust IPO pipeline despite global uncertainties and volatility.

Investors also anticipated the historic market debut of Elon Musk's SpaceX, expected to be Wall Street's largest public listing ever.
